sourceMovie.title: Thor
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Thor for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kenneth Branagh keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Doctor Strange
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Thor who respond to its adventure will find Doctor Strange (2016) immediately familiar. Both films share a fantasy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Thor: The Dark World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Thor fans keep landing on Thor: The Dark World (2013) — the adventure is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings draws from the same adventure well as Thor, channeling fantasy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2021 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness (2022) captures the adventure appeal of Thor without simply imitating it. The fantasy streak running through both films is what puts Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Avengers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The adventure appeal of Thor — that tense edge, the action — translates cleanly into The Avengers (2012). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Infinity War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Avengers: Infinity War (2018) clearly understood what makes adventure work — the same quality that keeps Thor rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) is the kind of film Thor fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The adventure overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Captain America: Civil War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Captain America: Civil War shares Thor's gift for adventure — the 2016 film delivers action without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Black Panther
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Thor compelling — the adventure, the action — shows up in Black Panther (2018) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The adventure energy of Thor is alive in Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 (2017), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thor?
faq.answer: Try Doctor Strange, Thor: The Dark World, Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings—each shares DNA with Thor while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thor?
faq.answer: Thor (2011) is best described as Adventure, Fantasy, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thor part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Thor Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
